channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Flytipped sofa and chair, 19th March
item.description:

item.title: 2+ Flags on Howard Avenue, 19th March
item.description:
In the early hours of the morning on 19.3.26 at least two Union Jack flags were attached to lampposts on Howard Avenue.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 88, Howard Avenue, Blendon, Greater London

item.title: Armchair, carpet., 19th March
item.description:
They have been here for a few weeks now, and i've seen rats near them.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Maynard Close, North End, Greater London

item.title: Uncollected recycling waste, 19th March
item.description:
Recycling waste has not been collected again. Remains on street blocking footpath. Passersby now dropping rubbish (empty bottles, ciagrette packets, dog excrement) on top of it. Please arrange urgent collection.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Barbauld Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dog waste here, 19th March
item.description:
Please remove

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Villa Street, Walworth, Greater London

item.title: Dog waste here, 19th March
item.description:
Please remove

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Burton Grove, Walworth, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: 39 Harvist Road, 19th March
item.description:
The recycling bin serving on the building (a council property with multiple flats) has once again not been collected on the scheduled date. This is not an isolated incident, but part of an ongoing pattern of missed collections. This is causing inconvenience to residents, as bins are becoming full and waste is accumulating. I would appreciate it if you could: Arrange for an urgent collection of the recycling bin Investigate why collections are being repeatedly missed at this address Confirm what measures will be put in place to prevent this from happening again
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Harvist Road, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Large cardboard boxes dumped, 19th March
item.description:
And are now getting filled with rubbish.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Sandy Hill Road, Woolwich
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: A Fly-tip Large - More than 10 black bags problem has been found, 19th March
item.description:
A Fly-tip Large - More than 10 black bags problem has been found by Brent Council

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Chatsworth Road, London
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Fly-tip Large - More than a van load (Estates)

item.title: Newby place, e14; vast whole local junction with main east india dock road,, 19th March
item.description:
THIS LOOKS TO BE A LOCAL COUNCIL ROAD, BUT IT COMES UNDER THE GLA/Tfl RESPONSIBILITY, BECAUSE IT BUTTS A RED ROUTE, AND BECAUSE IT IS ALSO A Tfl BUS, TRUCK, AND OTHER VEHICLE RT ANGLE TURNING INTO IT. which grinds up the surface. BUS STOP 30M IN FROM A13.
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Res at 52 Izane Rd, Bexeyheath putting cones o/s on c/way, 19th March
item.description:
Res at 52 Izane Rd, Bexeyheath putting cones o/s on c/way - caller says this is making it hard for people to park along the road
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 91, Izane Road, Blendon, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: EXPANSE OF ROAD JUNCTION, WHERE BUSES AND TRUCKS TURN TO AVOID, OR MISSED EARLIER TRAFFIC LIGHTS. THE LAST 30metres, where the 2ndary road, meets, East India Dock Road. The whole area has lifted tarmac of cobbles. Ruts, Ridges, Extremely dangerous to all; especially to disabled, older persons, I am 80yrs. ROAD FOR PROBABLY THE WHOLE YEAR, has not been surveyed, as this is an ongoing serious safety issue, demanding class 1 deep repairs. Or the turning buses etc, will grind up the 30mm tarmac, and it will be back to start again., 19th March
item.description:
This is not a pothole, but it has been like nearly unrepairable for more than a 12months. See above - all details there;
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: 94 Brondesbury villas on pavement, 19th March
item.description:
White builders sack. Empty

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Brondesbury Villas, London
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Fly-tip Small - Less than one bag

item.title: Dumped, white fridge or freezer, 19th March
item.description:
This has just been dumped. Outside, 25 Stane Way Se184pe. on the grass.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Stane Way, Woolwich
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Outside 49 Heath St on Holly Hill, 19th March
item.description:
I have reported this twice now and the report has been updated by the council to ‘no further action’ (9117017). As per my last report, following the attendance of two council contractors two days ago, the root of the problem, which is a broken drain, has not been addressed. The cement the contractors poorly applied has already broken away. They have raised the paving slab to a height which now presents a very serious trip hazard. Unless this is addressed asap somebody could potentially injure themselves. Please ensure this is urgently and effectively repaired.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Heath Street, Belsize Park, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Pothol, 19th March
item.description:
2large potholes at entrance to the bridge over Tylney Road
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Page Heath Villas, Widmore, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Broken paving slabs all along Porthkerry Ave, Welling, 19th March
item.description:
Broken paving slabs all along Porthkerry Ave, Welling - elderly lady says she has already fallen and is worried about her husband falling over.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Porthkerry Avenue, Welling, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
